2603.14400.title: Extending Minimal Pairs with Ordinal Surprisal Curves and Entropy Across Applied Domains
2603.14400.paper_summary: The minimal pairs paradigm of comparing model probabilities for contrasting completions has proven useful for evaluating linguistic knowledge in language models, yet its application has largely been confined to binary grammaticality judgments over syntactic phenomena. Additionally, standard prompting-based evaluation requires expensive text generation, may elicit post-hoc rationalizations rather than model judgments, and discards information about model uncertainty. We address both limitations by extending surprisal-based evaluation from binary grammaticality contrasts to ordinal-scaled classification and scoring tasks across multiple domains. Rather than asking models to generate answers, we measure the information-theoretic "surprise" (negative log probability) they assign to each position on rating scales (e.g., 1-5 or 1-9), yielding full surprisal curves that reveal both the model's preferred response and its uncertainty via entropy. We explore this framework across four domains: social-ecological-technological systems classification, causal statement identification (binary and scaled), figurative language detection, and deductive qualitative coding. Across these domains, surprisal curves produce interpretable classification signals with clear minima near expected ordinal scale positions, and entropy over the completion tended to distinguish genuinely ambiguous items from easier items.
2603.14400.paper_summary_zh: 最小對偶範式比較模型概率以對比完成的方式已被證明對於評估語言模型中的語言知識非常有用，但其應用主要限於對語法現象的二元語法判斷。此外，基於標準提示的評估需要昂貴的文本生成，可能引發事後合理化而非模型判斷，並且丟棄有關模型不確定性的資訊。我們通過將基於驚訝度的評估從二元語法對比擴展到多個領域的序數標度分類和評分任務來解決這兩個限制。我們不要求模型生成答案，而是測量它們對評分尺度上每個位置所分配的信息論“驚訝”（負對數概率），產生完整的驚訝度曲線，揭示模型的首選反應及其通過熵表達的不確定性。我們在四個領域中探索這一框架：社會-生態-技術系統分類、因果陳述識別（二元和標度）、比喻語言檢測和演繹性質性編碼。在這些領域中，驚訝度曲線產生可解釋的分類信號，並在預期的序數尺度位置附近顯示出明確的最小值，而對於完成的熵則傾向於區分真正模糊的項目和較簡單的項目。
2603.14400.author: Andrew Katz et.al.
